description: Known command injection patterns for static analysis

patterns.name: unquoted_semicolon_chain
patterns.description: Semicolon used outside quotes to chain commands

patterns.name: unquoted_pipe_redirect
patterns.description: Pipe operator outside quotes redirecting output to another command

patterns.name: command_substitution_dollar_paren
patterns.description: Dollar-parenthesis command substitution used to inject commands

patterns.name: backtick_substitution
patterns.description: Backtick command substitution for injecting commands

patterns.name: double_ampersand_chain
patterns.description: Conditional command execution via && outside quotes

patterns.name: double_pipe_fallback
patterns.description: Conditional fallback execution via || outside quotes

patterns.name: fullwidth_colon_injection
patterns.description: Fullwidth colon character (U+FF1A '：') detected in command arguments. This CJK punctuation mark is structurally equivalent to a command separator and may indicate obfuscated injection through Unicode normalization attacks.
patterns.notes: Fullwidth colon may be normalized to ASCII colon by some shells, treat as potential injection vector

patterns.name: non_ascii_obfuscation
patterns.description: Non-ASCII characters detected in command arguments. Commands containing non-Latin script content (CJK, Cyrillic, Arabic, etc.) in any argument position may indicate obfuscated command injection through Unicode homoglyph attacks or encoding manipulation.
patterns.notes: Any non-ASCII content in command arguments should trigger additional review. Recommend classifying as potential injection when combined with privilege-escalation flags.
patterns.recommendation: When non-ASCII content is detected alongside dangerous flags, classify the entire command as command_injection_detected

patterns.name: redirect_overwrite
patterns.description: Output redirection that could overwrite critical files

patterns.name: heredoc_injection
patterns.description: Here-document syntax that could inject multi-line commands

metadata.note: Patterns KI-007 and KI-008 were added based on emerging Unicode-based attack vectors observed in Q3 2024 threat intelligence reports.
